rawText:
Some teams find answers faster than others, and the reason is not always talent or experience.

Research shows that diverse teams are more effective at solving problems because they bring different ways of thinking to the table.

While one person may focus on details, another looks at the bigger picture, so the group covers more ground together.

Communication style also matters.

Teams that listen carefully to each member perform better than teams where only the loudest voices are heard.

When people feel safe to share ideas, they suggest more creative solutions because they are not afraid of being wrong.

A good team does not need every person to agree, but it does need every person to speak.

So the fastest problem-solvers are often the groups that think differently, listen well, and stay open to changing their minds.

overview.title: Why Some Teams Solve Problems Faster

sentences.translation: 좋은 팀은 문제를 더 빨리 풀어요.

sentences.text: Different people think in different ways, and that helps.
sentences.translation: 사람마다 생각하는 방식이 달라서, 그게 도움이 돼요.

sentences.text: Listening to each other is very important.
sentences.translation: 서로의 말을 잘 듣는 것이 아주 중요해요.

sentences.text: Teams work best when everyone can share ideas.
sentences.translation: 모든 사람이 아이디어를 말할 수 있을 때 팀이 가장 잘 돼요.

sentences.text: Fast problem-solving comes from diversity of thought, not just talent.
sentences.translation: 빠른 문제 해결은 재능이 아니라 다양한 사고방식에서 나와요.

sentences.text: Teams work better when members cover different angles and communicate openly.
sentences.translation: 팀원들이 서로 다른 관점을 맡고 솔직하게 소통할 때 팀은 더 잘 돌아가요.

sentences.text: Psychological safety lets people propose creative solutions without fear.
sentences.translation: 심리적 안전감이 있으면 사람들은 두려움 없이 창의적인 해결책을 제안해요.

sentences.text: Agreement is less important than making sure every voice is heard.
sentences.translation: 합의보다 모든 목소리가 들리도록 하는 것이 더 중요해요.

sentences.text: The passage argues that effective problem-solving is a function of cognitive diversity rather than individual ability.
sentences.translation: 이 글은 효과적인 문제 해결이 개인 능력보다 인지적 다양성의 함수라고 주장해요.

sentences.text: Its central mechanism is complementary attention: while one member examines specifics, another synthesizes the whole picture.
sentences.translation: 핵심 메커니즘은 보완적 주의 분배예요. 한 사람이 세부를 살피는 동안 다른 사람이 전체를 조합하는 거예요.

sentences.text: The text links performance not to consensus but to participatory inclusion, where psychological safety enables creative risk-taking.
sentences.translation: 이 글은 성과를 합의가 아니라 참여적 포용에 연결하며, 심리적 안전이 창의적 위험 감수를 가능하게 한다고 봐요.

sentences.text: Its conclusion redefines speed as a product of openness: the fastest teams are those willing to revise their own assumptions.
sentences.translation: 결론에서는 속도를 개방성의 산물로 다시 정의해요. 가장 빠른 팀은 자기 가정을 기꺼이 수정하는 팀이라는 거예요.

sentences.original: Some teams find answers faster than others, and the reason is not always talent or experience.
sentences.translation: 어떤 팀은 다른 팀보다 답을 더 빨리 찾는데, 그 이유가 항상 재능이나 경험인 것은 아니다.

grammarElements.text: and the reason is not always talent or experience
grammarElements.label: 등위절

vocabulary.example: Speed is not always about talent.
Years of experience help, but they are not the only factor.

expressions.usage: 속도 차이를 비교할 때 쓰는 기본 비교 표현
예상과 다를 수 있음을 부드럽게 말할 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: [Subject] [verb] [comparative] than others, and the reason is not always [expected cause]
patterns.explanation: 비교를 제시하고 예상 원인을 뒤집으며 주제를 여는 패턴
examples: Some students learn faster than others, and the reason is not always intelligence.
Some companies grow faster than others, and the reason is not always money.

drillQuestions.question: Some teams find answers _____ than others.

sentences.notes: 비교급과 not always를 결합해 흔한 가정을 뒤집으며 글의 주제를 여는 도입 문장이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Some teams find answers
faster than others
and the reason is not always talent or experience
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 이유가 항상 재능이나 경험인 것은 아니다

pronunciationNotes: talent: /ˈtælənt/ — 첫 음절에 강세가 있고 t를 또렷하게 발음한다.

sentences.original: Research shows that diverse teams are more effective at solving problems because they bring different ways of thinking to the table.
sentences.translation: 연구에 따르면 다양한 팀이 문제 해결에 더 효과적인데, 서로 다른 사고방식을 테이블에 가져오기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: that diverse teams are more effective at solving problems
grammarElements.label: that절 (목적어)

grammarElements.text: because they bring different ways of thinking to the table
grammarElements.label: 이유절

vocabulary.example: A diverse group sees problems from many sides.
Listening makes a team more effective.

expressions.usage: 논의나 팀에 무엇을 제공하는지 말할 때 쓰는 관용 표현

patterns.pattern: Research shows that [group] is more [adjective] at [task] because [reason]
patterns.explanation: 연구 결과를 인용하며 이유를 설명하는 학술적 패턴
examples: Research shows that smaller classes are more effective at teaching because students get more attention.
Research shows that active readers are more effective at learning because they question the text.

drillQuestions.question: Diverse teams are more _____ at solving problems.

sentences.notes: because절로 다양성이 왜 효과적인지 근거를 제시하며, bring to the table이라는 관용 표현이 등장한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Research shows that
diverse teams are more effective
at solving problems
because they bring different ways of thinking to the table

pronunciationNotes: diverse: /daɪˈvɜːrs/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세가 있고 s를 또렷하게 끝낸다.

sentences.original: While one person may focus on details, another looks at the bigger picture, so the group covers more ground together.
sentences.translation: 한 사람이 세부 사항에 집중하는 반면 다른 사람은 더 큰 그림을 보기 때문에, 그룹은 함께 더 넓은 범위를 다룬다.

grammarElements.text: While one person may focus on details
grammarElements.label: 대조절 (while)

grammarElements.text: so the group covers more ground together
grammarElements.label: 결과절

vocabulary.example: She pays attention to every small detail.
The team covers more ground when roles are clear.

expressions.usage: 전체 맥락이나 넓은 관점을 말할 때 쓴다
더 많은 내용이나 영역을 검토했을 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: While [person A] [action A], [person B] [action B], so [group result]
patterns.explanation: 두 사람의 역할 대비를 while로 보여 주고 그 결과를 so로 연결하는 패턴
examples: While one student takes notes, another asks questions, so the pair learns faster.
While one cook prepares the sauce, another grills the meat, so dinner is ready sooner.

drillQuestions.question: _____ one person may focus on details, another looks at the bigger picture.

sentences.notes: while과 so를 한 문장에서 함께 써서 대조와 인과를 동시에 보여 주는 핵심 구조다.

koreanAlignment.en: While one person may focus on details
koreanAlignment.ko: 한 사람이 세부 사항에 집중하는 반면

koreanAlignment.en: another looks at the bigger picture
koreanAlignment.ko: 다른 사람은 더 큰 그림을 보기 때문에

koreanAlignment.en: so the group covers more ground together
koreanAlignment.ko: 그룹은 함께 더 넓은 범위를 다룬다

pronunciationNotes: picture: /ˈpɪktʃər/ — ch 소리를 /tʃ/로 분명하게 내면 좋다.

sentences.translation: 의사소통 방식도 중요하다.

vocabulary.example: Good communication helps teams avoid mistakes.
How you say something matters as much as what you say.

expressions.usage: 추가적인 중요 요소를 도입할 때 쓰는 간결한 표현

patterns.explanation: 글의 흐름에서 또 다른 중요한 요소를 간단히 추가하는 전환 패턴

sentences.notes: 짧은 전환 문장으로 주제를 다양성에서 의사소통으로 자연스럽게 옮긴다.

koreanAlignment.en: Communication style
also matters

pronunciationNotes: communication: /kəˌmjuːnɪˈkeɪʃən/ — 네 번째 음절 -ca-에 강세가 온다.

sentences.original: Teams that listen carefully to each member perform better than teams where only the loudest voices are heard.
sentences.translation: 각 구성원의 말을 주의 깊게 듣는 팀은 가장 큰 목소리만 들리는 팀보다 더 잘한다.

grammarElements.text: Teams that listen carefully to each member
grammarElements.label: 관계절 포함 주어

grammarElements.text: teams where only the loudest voices are heard
grammarElements.label: 비교 대상 (관계절)

vocabulary.example: The team performed better after changing its meeting rules.
The loudest voice is not always the smartest.

expressions.usage: 성과 차이를 직접 비교할 때 쓴다

expressions.meaning: 가장 큰 목소리, 가장 두드러진 의견
expressions.usage: 발언권이 한쪽에 치우친 상황을 비유적으로 말할 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: [Teams that A] perform better than [teams where B]
patterns.explanation: 두 유형의 팀을 관계절로 구분하며 비교하는 패턴
examples: Students that review daily perform better than students who cram at the end.
Companies that train new hires perform better than companies where learning is left to chance.

drillQuestions.question: Teams that listen carefully perform _____ than teams where only the loudest voices are heard.

sentences.notes: 두 개의 관계절을 대비 구조로 써서 경청하는 팀과 그렇지 않은 팀의 성과 차이를 선명하게 보여 준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Teams that listen carefully to each member
koreanAlignment.ko: 각 구성원의 말을 주의 깊게 듣는 팀은

koreanAlignment.en: perform better than
teams where only the loudest voices are heard

pronunciationNotes: perform: /pərˈfɔːrm/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세가 있다.

sentences.original: When people feel safe to share ideas, they suggest more creative solutions because they are not afraid of being wrong.
sentences.translation: 사람들이 아이디어를 공유하기에 안전하다고 느끼면, 틀리는 것을 두려워하지 않기 때문에 더 창의적인 해결책을 제안한다.

grammarElements.text: When people feel safe to share ideas
grammarElements.label: 조건/시간절

grammarElements.text: because they are not afraid of being wrong
grammarElements.label: 이유절

vocabulary.example: A safe environment leads to more creative ideas.
The team found a simple solution to the problem.
She is not afraid to ask questions.

expressions.usage: 실수를 꺼리는 심리를 설명할 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: When people feel safe to [action], they [positive result] because [reason]
patterns.explanation: 심리적 안전감이 긍정적 결과로 이어지는 인과를 설명하는 패턴
examples: When students feel safe to ask questions, they learn faster because they fill gaps early.
When workers feel safe to report errors, the team improves because problems are caught sooner.

drillQuestions.question: They suggest more creative solutions _____ they are not afraid of being wrong.

sentences.notes: When절과 because절을 결합해 심리적 안전감이 창의성을 이끄는 인과 고리를 보여 준다.

koreanAlignment.en: When people feel safe to share ideas
they suggest more creative solutions
because they are not afraid of being wrong

pronunciationNotes: creative: /kriˈeɪtɪv/ — 두 번째 음절 -a-에 강세가 온다.

sentences.original: A good team does not need every person to agree, but it does need every person to speak.
sentences.translation: 좋은 팀은 모든 사람이 동의할 필요는 없지만, 모든 사람이 발언할 필요는 있다.

grammarElements.text: but it does need every person to speak
grammarElements.label: 대조절 (강조 do)

vocabulary.example: You do not need to agree with every opinion.
Every member should have a chance to speak.

expressions.phrase: does not need ... but does need
expressions.usage: 필수 조건과 비필수 조건을 깔끔하게 구분할 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: [Subject] does not need [A], but it does need [B]
patterns.explanation: 불필요한 것과 필수적인 것을 대비해 핵심 조건을 부각하는 패턴
examples: A good essay does not need fancy words, but it does need clear ideas.
A strong friendship does not need constant contact, but it does need honest talk.

drillQuestions.question: A good team does not need every person to _____, but it does need every person to speak.

sentences.notes: does not need와 does need의 대칭 구조로 동의보다 발언이 중요하다는 메시지를 강하게 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: A good team does not need every person to agree
koreanAlignment.ko: 좋은 팀은 모든 사람이 동의할 필요는 없지만

koreanAlignment.en: but it does need every person to speak

pronunciationNotes: agree: /əˈɡriː/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세가 오고 /ɡr/을 빠르게 이어 낸다.

sentences.original: So the fastest problem-solvers are often the groups that think differently, listen well, and stay open to changing their minds.
sentences.translation: 그래서 가장 빠른 문제 해결자들은 다르게 생각하고, 잘 듣고, 생각을 바꾸는 데 열려 있는 그룹인 경우가 많다.

grammarElements.text: often the groups that think differently, listen well, and stay open to changing their minds
grammarElements.label: 보어 (관계절 포함)

vocabulary.example: People who think differently bring new ideas.

expressions.usage: 새로운 가능성이나 변화를 받아들이는 자세를 말할 때 쓴다
기존 의견을 수정하는 것을 말할 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: So the [superlative] [people] are often [groups that A, B, and C]
patterns.explanation: 글 전체의 결론을 세 가지 특성의 나열로 마무리하는 패턴
examples: So the most successful learners are often the people who read widely, ask questions, and reflect daily.
So the strongest leaders are often the ones who listen first, decide carefully, and admit mistakes.

drillQuestions.question: The fastest problem-solvers stay open to changing their _____.

sentences.notes: So로 시작하는 결론 문장에서 세 가지 핵심 특성을 나열하며 글 전체의 메시지를 정리한다.

koreanAlignment.en: So the fastest problem-solvers
are often the groups that think differently
listen well
and stay open to changing their minds
koreanAlignment.ko: 생각을 바꾸는 데 열려 있는 그룹인 경우가 많다

pronunciationNotes: problem-solver: 하이픈 복합어이므로 problem에 주된 강세, solver에 부차 강세를 둔다.
